rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Judgment Creditors Notice Of Motion In Support Of Objection And Objection To The Form Substance And Amount And Sufficiency Thereof Of The Judgment Debtors Undertaking Served June 10 2019
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 18, 2019 line 1. PLAINTIFF DALE DUNCAN, MARTA MUNOZ MENDOZA Judgment Creditors Notice Of Motion In Support Of Objection And Objection To The Form Substance And Amount And Sufficiency Thereof Of The Judgment Debtors Undertaking Served June 10 2019 Hearing Required. The Court notes that the supplemental briefing requested by the Court failed to address the specific issues the Court sought to be addressed, i.e. how exactly the judgment creditor may collect on each interest in each property by each surety. Instead, the supplemental brief (1) recited the general law on collection against sureties without connecting it to any particular interest by any particular surety in any particular property real or personal; and (2) re-listed the interests, information that has been provided several times before, i.e. the Court was not looking for it to be provided again. The request for supplemental briefing was indicated in the tentative ruling and no clarification (in case the directions were unclear) was asked for during the hearing. The Court received the declarations regarding appraisers and will make a determination regarding an appointment, if necessary. All sureties to be present in the event the Court concludes that an evidentiary hearing is required. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: VERLIANT ENERGY, INC. VS. DAKOTA NOTE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief Fromentry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 18, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT KEITH KNAPP Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief Fromentry Of Default DENIED without prejudice. As to CCP 473(d) Defendant fails to explain how a review of the judgment roll, including the Proof of Service on which the default is based, would show that as a matter of law (i.e. citation to cases) the default is improper for lack of jurisdiction. Defendant quotes CCP 415.20 and proceeds to conclude without any analysis of fact or law that service in this case was improper. Defendant failed to cite any cases for the proposition that under the circumstances of this case equitable relief is appropriate. McCreadie v. Arques (1967) 248 Cal.App.2d 39 is factually distinguishable. Finally, under CCP 473(b) Defendant failed to show that the default was due to mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. The Court notes that Defendant's declaration (1) does not deny that the address of service was his mailing address at the relevant time; (2) does not deny the receipt of the mailed copies of the summons and complaint; (3) does not deny that the address at issue was listed with a professional licensing authority and has not been changed to provide different information; (4) does not deny that he had knowledge of this action. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN LEE ET AL VS. 24TH & HOFFMAN INVESTORS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 18, 2019 line 7. Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint GRANTED. Plaintiff to file amended/supplemental complaint no later than July 19, 2019 and serve it forthwith. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: COLETH EGU VS. SMITH AWAMBU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 18, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT SMITH AWAMBU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly allege whether property is subject to SFRO (eviction and/or rent control); whether it is exempt from all provisions of SFRO, i.e. is not subject to either eviction or rent control. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL FRANK GERALDI FAMILY II, LLC VS. ROBERT GELARDI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 18, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT ROBERT GELARDI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts providing satisfactory explanation for changed factual allegations. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that (potentially damaging) allegations in a verified complaint may be changed by amendment without any explanation. = (501/CFH)
